title: Attention Layers Inside the U-Net
concept: Modern diffusion U-Nets add self-attention layers (callback to Unit 258) at certain resolutions, helping the model reason about long-range image structure.

exercises.prompt: Quick warm-up: What does 'self-attention' help a model do (callback to Unit 258)?

options.text: Relate distant parts of the input to each other
Make the image smaller in file size

exercises.explain: Self-attention lets every part of the input 'look at' every other part, so the model can connect things that are far apart, like two eyes on opposite sides of a face.

cards.title: The U-Net Needs Eyes Everywhere
cards.body: Convolutions are great at looking at small neighborhoods of pixels, but what if the left ear of a cat needs to match the right ear far away in the image? That's where attention layers step in!

cards.title: What Are Attention Layers in a U-Net?
cards.body: At certain resolutions inside the U-Net, self-attention layers are inserted between the convolution blocks. They let every pixel-patch 'compare notes' with every other pixel-patch, helping the model keep long-range structure consistent, like symmetric faces or matching patterns across the whole image.

cards.title: The Group Chat Analogy
cards.body: Convolutions are like each puzzle piece only talking to its close neighbors. Self-attention is like putting all the puzzle pieces in one big group chat, so any piece can message any other piece directly, even ones far away, to make sure the whole picture fits together.

cards.title: Keeping Symmetry Straight
cards.body: Imagine denoising an image of a butterfly. The left wing pattern needs to match the right wing pattern, even though they're on opposite sides of the image. An attention layer lets the U-Net directly connect those two far-apart regions so the wings end up matching.

cards.title: Myth: Attention Replaces Convolutions
cards.body: Nope! Modern diffusion U-Nets use BOTH. Convolutions handle local detail efficiently, while attention layers are added at select resolutions (usually smaller, lower-res stages) to handle long-range relationships. It's teamwork, not a replacement.

cards.title: Local Convolution vs. Global Attention
cards.body: Convolutions scan nearby pixels only; attention layers let any region connect to any other region, no matter the distance.

left.text: Only looks at a small nearby patch of pixels, like whispering to your neighbor

right.text: Connects every region to every other region, like a global group chat

exercises.prompt: Why do modern diffusion U-Nets add self-attention layers at certain resolutions?

options.text: To help the model reason about long-range image structure
To remove the need for a decoder
To shrink the image to one pixel

exercises.explain: Self-attention layers help connect distant parts of the image, which is something local convolutions alone struggle to do.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Diffusion U-Nets use self-attention INSTEAD of convolutions, not alongside them.
exercises.explain: False! U-Nets combine both: convolutions for local detail and attention layers at certain resolutions for long-range structure.

exercises.sentence: Self-attention layers help the model reason about ___ image structure.
exercises.explain: Self-attention connects far-apart regions of the image, which is why it helps with long-range structure like symmetry and global patterns.

exercises.prompt: Which resolution stages of the U-Net typically include self-attention layers?

options.text: Certain resolutions, often the lower-resolution (smaller, more compressed) stages
Every single pixel at full resolution, always
Only the very first input layer before any processing

exercises.explain: Attention is usually added at select resolutions, often smaller/compressed stages, where it's computationally efficient to compare all regions to each other.

exercises.prompt: Boss Challenge: A diffusion U-Net is generating a symmetric flower image, but petals on opposite sides don't match. Which architectural feature would best help fix this by connecting far-apart regions of the image?

options.text: Adding self-attention layers at certain resolutions
Using only convolutions with smaller kernels
Increasing the noise level added to the image

exercises.explain: Self-attention layers let distant regions of the image directly communicate, which is exactly what's needed to keep long-range patterns like symmetric petals consistent.
